Title: Re: Replacement for Robert McKee
Notes:
Ticket Note:

Received a request from Donald for the creation of a new user, Juan (email: juanc@jdhpacific.com).

  1. Active Directory (AD) Setup:
    • Logged into the Active Directory (AD) server and created the user Juan.
    • After creating the user, I initiated the synchronization to ensure that all details were properly updated across the systems.
  2. Office 365 Setup:
    • Subsequently, I logged into Office 365 to confirm that the user was properly created and that the required license was applied.
    • I checked the user’s mailbox and ensured everything was set up correctly. Once I confirmed the setup, I proceeded with the next steps.
  3. License Assignment:
    • Since the user did not have a license initially, I assigned the appropriate Office 365 license to Juan.
  4. Credential Delivery:
    • Once everything was set up, I converted the Robert email account to a shared mailbox after that  then sent the juan's credentials to Donald in an encrypted form for security purposes.
  5. Final Confirmation:
    • After confirming that the user account was properly set up and the license was assigned, I informed Donald of the successful creation and activation of the user account.

Title: JDH-HQ-PC-06 : Blackout screen, unable to access
Notes:
Attempted to access JDH-HQ-PC-06 via MyRemote but was initially unable to connect. Then accessed the system using ManageEngine, after which access was successful.

Uninstalled the older version of MyRemote and installed the latest version. The system was still slow, so services were optimized and an SFC scan was run. After completion, the system was rebooted.

Post-restart, the system was accessible via MyRemote and is now working smoothly as expected. Also notes also updated with hans at myremote over this pc.
Resource:
S. Kumar Sharma
CHD-TS1
Support - Remote

Title: DESKTOP-KND184L : Patch update failed due to low disk space,need cleanup immediately
Notes:
Manually accessed the machine and performed a standard system cleanup to improve available disk space.

The cleanup activities included removing temporary files, clearing system cache, deleting unnecessary user and system-generated files, and optimizing storage usage. Additionally, unused or obsolete files were identified and removed to free up space.

As a result of these actions, approximately 9 GB of disk space was successfully recovered, improving overall system performance and stability.
Resource:
S. Kumar Sharma
CHD-TS1
Support - Remote

Title: QNAP Firmware update
Notes: I logged into the QNAP system in the JDH environment and found that firmware updates were pending. Before proceeding with the update, I verified that all backup jobs had completed successfully. Since a reboot was required prior to installing the firmware, I rebooted the QNAP device, installed the updates, and then performed a second reboot to finalize the installation. After the system came back online, I verified that all shared folders were accessible. I then re-scanned the backup repository, and the scan completed successfully.

Title: update missing patches on SupplySalesconf,JDH-REMOT-PC-01,JDH-HQ-PC-05 JDH-HQ-PC-03
Notes:
Devices SupplySalesconf and JDH-REMOT-PC-01 were identified as non-compliant due to missing patches.

Logged in to the ManageEngine portal and verified pending updates on both systems. Proceeded with patch remediation by installing the following updates:

  • .NET Desktop Runtime (x64) – Version 8.0.26 (KB5086096)
  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 147.0.7727.101 / 147.0.7727.102
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Business (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2603 (Build 19822.20182)

After installation, performed a manual patch scan on both systems to verify successful deployment and to clear the non-compliant status.

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. Both systems are now compliant as per patch management policy.

Title: Re: Parallels password reset for Nancy
Notes:
Following up on the recent tasks and discussions with Donald, the following issues have been successfully addressed:

  1. Sage Password and Application Password:
    • The Sage password and Application password have been provided as requested, and access has been granted.
  2. Calendar and Microsoft 365 in Combined Mode:
    • The request to combine the Calendar and Microsoft 365 calendars has been successfully implemented. Both calendars are now visible in a single, merged view.
  3. Outlook Synchronization:
    • The Outlook synchronization process has been completed successfully. All Outlook data has been fully synced and is now up-to-date.
  4. Adobe Issue:
    • The Adobe Acrobat extension issue was resolved, and the extension has been installed successfully. A computer restart was recommended, and it has been confirmed that the system is now functioning properly.

Current Status:

  • Sage and Application Passwords: Provided and confirmed.
  • Calendar and Microsoft 365 Integration: Successfully merged.
  • Outlook Synchronization: Completed successfully, all data synced.
  • Adobe Issue: Resolved; system functioning fine after restart.

Action Taken:

  • All issues have been addressed, and the customer was notified that everything is fixed.
  • The computer restart to finalize the Adobe installation was completed successfully.

Customer Communication:

  • The customer, Donald, was informed that all issues have now been resolved, including the Adobe issue, calendar integration, and Outlook synchronization.
  • Donald was updated about the completion of all tasks and was thanked for his patience during the process.
  • He was reminded that if any further issues arise or if he needs assistance, he can contact us at any time.

Title: Storage warning alarm on BKP_MD_JDH
Notes: Logged into QNAP storage for BKP_MD_JDH and then start cleaning up the space on the qnap. Tried to delete old snapshots and old data from the storage LUN to clear the space on LUN.  but there was nothing to delete so increased the storage LUN space by 1TB. After that re-scan the storage on the LUN and then verify the storage location has clear all warning alerts from it.

Title: Issue with Intermittent ScreenConnect Disconnections- JDH-HQ-PC-06 for Hans Wang
Notes: Received a call on the support number from Hans Wang, who reported intermittent disconnections of the ScreenConnect session on machine JDH-HQ-PC-06. The issue occurred when using the copy command, causing a 3-5 second disruption. The connection worked fine when checked via ManageEngine. After analyzing the Event Viewer logs, I identified a SocketException error (Event ID 20), indicating a failure in establishing a connection to the ScreenConnect server, and an application crash. I reinstalled the ScreenConnect agent on the user's machine, but the issue persisted. I then ran the SFC/Scannow command to check for any system file corruption, updated the Office apps on the user's machine, and asked the user to confirm if the issue was resolved. After these steps, the user confirmed that the ScreenConnect session was functioning correctly without further disconnections.
